title: Expose DevMesh runtime status through MCP
summary: DevMesh now provides a mesh_get_status MCP tool so Codex and other MCP clients can verify the running DevMesh version, mode, project root/store, daemon details, and knowledge counts before assuming their configuration is correct. Runtime version reporting is centralized through DEV_MESH_VERSION and release version checks now verify that constant against the root and CLI package versions.

title: Release DevMesh 0.1.2
summary: [done] Released DevMesh 0.1.2 by bumping synchronized workspace/CLI/runtime versions, validating with pnpm release:check, committing c7adff0, tagging v0.1.2, and pushing main plus the tag. GitHub Actions NPM Publish and Release Artifacts both completed successfully; npm dist-tag alpha now points to devmesh@0.1.2 and GitHub prerelease v0.1.2 is published.

title: Website Pages deployment blocked by repository Pages setting
summary: [blocked] Attempted to deploy the DevMesh VitePress website through the Website Pages workflow. The build job and Pages artifact upload succeeded, but the deploy job failed with GitHub Pages deployment 404: Pages must be enabled for xy200303/DevMesh and Source must be set to GitHub Actions. Local environment has no GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN, so repository settings cannot be changed programmatically from this session. After enabling Pages in Settings > Pages, rerun Website Pages or push a new main commit.

title: Website docs should use published DevMesh CLI install
summary: DevMesh website docs must avoid local source paths or workspace-only commands and should present the published npm CLI flow for users.
content: Updated website docs to remove leaked local Windows paths/source workspace commands and document the install-first flow: `npm install -g devmesh@alpha`, `dmx init`, `dmx status`, `dmx doctor`, `dmx capture`, `dmx search`, and optional team sync via `dmx join`. Verify future website edits with a leak scan for local path tokens and source-dev command examples before publishing.

title: Redesign website homepage and complete interface reference
summary: [done] Redesigned the DevMesh website homepage into a plain docs-style entry and added complete CLI, MCP, and HTTP API reference documentation. Pushed commit 8855e9c.
content: Updated the website homepage to remove the poster-like centered hero and use a restrained left-aligned docs entry with install command, common commands, command groups, and interface links. Expanded `reference/cli.md` into a full command and option reference. Added `reference/mcp.md` covering all 14 MCP tools and input fields. Added `reference/http.md` covering Hub discovery, join, sync, project, admin, and `/mcp` endpoints. Updated VitePress sidebar navigation. Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, hidden leak scan for local paths/dev commands, Playwright homepage screenshot at 1440x900, and pushed commit 8855e9c. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, a hidden leak scan over website docs/dist, and a search confirming the command appears in the built homepage. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

title: Website docs should use published DevMesh CLI install
summary: DevMesh website docs must avoid local source paths or workspace-only commands and should present the published npm CLI flow for users.
content: Updated website docs to remove leaked local Windows paths/source workspace commands and document the install-first flow: `npm install -g devmesh@alpha`, `dmx init`, `dmx status`, `dmx doctor`, `dmx capture`, `dmx search`, and optional team sync via `dmx join`. Verify future website edits with a leak scan for local path tokens and source-dev command examples before publishing.

title: Improve DevMesh website SEO and homepage polish
summary: [done] [done] Added VitePress SEO metadata, sitemap config, robots.txt, OG preview assets, and refined the homepage with a cleaner hero, terminal preview, setup flow, command/API entry cards, and responsive styling. Verified build, sitemap/robots/meta output, leak scan, desktop/mobile screenshots, then committed and pushed 3d4797c.

title: Expose DevMesh runtime status through MCP
summary: DevMesh now provides a mesh_get_status MCP tool so Codex and other MCP clients can verify the running DevMesh version, mode, project root/store, daemon details, and knowledge counts before assuming their configuration is correct. Runtime version reporting is centralized through DEV_MESH_VERSION and release version checks now verify that constant against the root and CLI package versions.

title: Add knowledge CRUD MCP tools
summary: [done] [done] Added explicit get/list/update/delete MCP tools for knowledge entries, wired them through core, local-store events, runtime, local stdio/HTTP proxy, server MCP, and tests. Verification passed for package typechecks, builds, and focused vitest suites. Committed as 0f38b86.

title: DevMesh 0.1.3 alpha published
summary: DevMesh v0.1.3 was released to npm with the alpha dist-tag and tagged as v0.1.3 on GitHub.
content: Release commit 64647f6 prepares DevMesh 0.1.3 by synchronizing package.json, apps/dmx/package.json, and DEV_MESH_VERSION, refreshing the README hero into a centered badge layout, and updating the shared SVG logo. Verification passed with pnpm build:website, git diff --check, and pnpm release:check. The v0.1.3 tag was pushed to GitHub and npm confirmed devmesh@0.1.3 with alpha pointing to 0.1.3.

title: README should stay product-facing and bilingual
summary: DevMesh root README should be a concise product entrypoint, with README.md in Chinese and README-EN.md in English, while detailed CLI/API/development material lives in website docs, CONTRIBUTING.md, and release docs.
content: Commit b9897c3 rewrote the root README into a product-facing overview: install command, local-first value, assistant-led MCP capture model, shareable .dev-mesh knowledge files, core commands, interface links, and security notes. It added README-EN.md with parallel English content, CONTRIBUTING.md for development and knowledge-store policy, and .github/pull_request_template.md for PR expectations. Avoid reintroducing long internal implementation dumps into README; link to devmesh.xyun.dev, docs/release.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md instead.

title: Avoid npm download badges for fresh package launches
summary: DevMesh README should avoid npm monthly download badges during early releases because the npm downloads API can legitimately report 0 for a new package and make the product look broken or inactive.
content: The npm downloads API returned 0 for last-month devmesh downloads immediately after the 0.1.3 alpha publish, and Shields rendered the README badge as `0/month`. Replaced the downloads badge in README.md and README-EN.md with a stable docs badge pointing to devmesh.xyun.dev. Prefer version, docs, platform, and compatibility badges over download counters until there is sustained package traffic.

title: Datawhale submission prep needs repo metadata, README structure, and proposal draft
summary: Preparing DevMesh for Datawhale DOPMC requires repository-level materials beyond core product docs: a software license, a code-of-conduct adoption file, README sections for audience/progress/team/license, and a ready-to-submit DOPMC proposal draft.
content: The Datawhale prep pass added MIT licensing, a local CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md that adopts the Datawhale DOPMC conduct principles, README and README-EN sections for audience, online reading, progress, project team, contribution flow, license, and Datawhale plan, plus docs/datawhale-dopmc-application.md as a proposal issue draft. The main remaining non-file task is to rename the GitHub repository to a lowercase form such as devmesh or dev-mesh before submitting to Datawhale.

title: Datawhale proposal draft should mirror issue body structure
summary: The DevMesh Datawhale DOPMC application draft should be written as a near-final issue body, not as generic notes, using sections such as guide acceptance, code-of-conduct acceptance, project intro, reasons, audience, highlights, completed work, roadmap, owner, and submission notes.
content: docs/datawhale-dopmc-application.md was tightened from a general planning memo into an issue-ready application draft. The structure now mirrors how Datawhale proposal issues are typically read: explicit acceptance of the guide and code of conduct, project intro, proposal reasons, target audience, project highlights, completed work, roadmap, owner, and final notes such as repository rename and contact-registration reminders.

title: Expose DevMesh runtime status through MCP
summary: DevMesh now provides a mesh_get_status MCP tool so Codex and other MCP clients can verify the running DevMesh version, mode, project root/store, daemon details, and knowledge counts before assuming their configuration is correct. Runtime version reporting is centralized through DEV_MESH_VERSION and release version checks now verify that constant against the root and CLI package versions.

title: Redesign website homepage and complete interface reference
summary: [done] Redesigned the DevMesh website homepage into a plain docs-style entry and added complete CLI, MCP, and HTTP API reference documentation. Pushed commit 8855e9c.
content: Updated the website homepage to remove the poster-like centered hero and use a restrained left-aligned docs entry with install command, common commands, command groups, and interface links. Expanded `reference/cli.md` into a full command and option reference. Added `reference/mcp.md` covering all 14 MCP tools and input fields. Added `reference/http.md` covering Hub discovery, join, sync, project, admin, and `/mcp` endpoints. Updated VitePress sidebar navigation. Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, hidden leak scan for local paths/dev commands, Playwright homepage screenshot at 1440x900, and pushed commit 8855e9c. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

title: Website Pages deployment blocked by repository Pages setting
summary: [blocked] Attempted to deploy the DevMesh VitePress website through the Website Pages workflow. The build job and Pages artifact upload succeeded, but the deploy job failed with GitHub Pages deployment 404: Pages must be enabled for xy200303/DevMesh and Source must be set to GitHub Actions. Local environment has no GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN, so repository settings cannot be changed programmatically from this session. After enabling Pages in Settings > Pages, rerun Website Pages or push a new main commit.

title: GitHub repository path is now xy200303/dev-mesh
summary: DevMesh now uses the GitHub repository path https://github.com/xy200303/dev-mesh. Repository-facing metadata and docs should point to the lowercase dev-mesh repo path while keeping the product name DevMesh, npm package name devmesh, and website domain devmesh.xyun.dev unchanged.
content: The repository was renamed from the mixed-case DevMesh form to the lowercase dev-mesh form on GitHub. Update repository.url in the CLI package, website GitHub links, issue links, and proposal/readme wording that previously said the rename was still pending. This rename does not change the product brand DevMesh, the npm package name devmesh, or the published docs domain devmesh.xyun.dev.

title: Website uses Lumen components with flattened home overrides
summary: DevMesh website now integrates @theojs/lumen for VitePress theming, cards, links, notice, aside, and footer, while overriding the default Lumen home gradient and glow so the homepage stays minimal and docs-like.
content: The website theme entry now imports @theojs/lumen styles and registers its VitePress components (Notice, Aside, Footer, Card, Links, Pill, CopyText, Underline). The homepage was rewritten to VitePress home layout and uses Lumen components for install, flow, reference, interface, and command sections. custom.css intentionally overrides Lumen's home hero gradient, glow, and image effects to preserve DevMesh's restrained visual style rather than adopting a flashy landing-page aesthetic.

title: Homepage hero uses a restrained two-column docs layout
summary: DevMesh website homepage now balances the hero with a left-aligned product message and a right-side operational summary card instead of leaving the right half empty or using decorative imagery.
content: The homepage hero now stays minimal but uses the VitePress home image slot for a practical panel: published CLI install command, MCP host/runtime/storage/team-sync facts, and quick links to getting started, MCP tools, and HTTP API. The left side remains a product-facing docs hero, but brand and category lines are separated typographically so the headline reads more like a mature documentation product than a marketing landing page.

Verify future website edits with a leak scan for local path tokens and source-dev command examples before publishing.

title: Improve DevMesh website SEO and homepage polish
summary: [done] [done] Added VitePress SEO metadata, sitemap config, robots.txt, OG preview assets, and refined the homepage with a cleaner hero, terminal preview, setup flow, command/API entry cards, and responsive styling. Verified build, sitemap/robots/meta output, leak scan, desktop/mobile screenshots, then committed and pushed 3d4797c.

title: Revert website to original VitePress style
summary: [done] Rolled back the DevMesh website from the recent Lumen-based homepage/theme restyle to the earlier native VitePress presentation, keeping current docs content but restoring the original homepage structure, theme wiring, footer, and package dependencies. Verified with pnpm build:website.

title: Website uses Lumen components with flattened home overrides
summary: DevMesh website now integrates @theojs/lumen for VitePress theming, cards, links, notice, aside, and footer, while overriding the default Lumen home gradient and glow so the homepage stays minimal and docs-like.
content: The website theme entry now imports @theojs/lumen styles and registers its VitePress components (Notice, Aside, Footer, Card, Links, Pill, CopyText, Underline). The homepage was rewritten to VitePress home layout and uses Lumen components for install, flow, reference, interface, and command sections. custom.css intentionally overrides Lumen's home hero gradient, glow, and image effects to preserve DevMesh's restrained visual style rather than adopting a flashy landing-page aesthetic.

title: Add published install command to website homepage
summary: [done] Added a visible `npm install -g devmesh@alpha` command strip to the DevMesh website homepage and pushed commit 6595278.
content: Changed `apps/website/docs/index.md` and `apps/website/docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css` to place the published npm install command directly in the first viewport. Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, a hidden leak scan over website docs/dist, and a search confirming the command appears in the built homepage. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

title: Redesign website homepage and complete interface reference
summary: [done] Redesigned the DevMesh website homepage into a plain docs-style entry and added complete CLI, MCP, and HTTP API reference documentation. Pushed commit 8855e9c.
content: Updated the website homepage to remove the poster-like centered hero and use a restrained left-aligned docs entry with install command, common commands, command groups, and interface links. Expanded `reference/cli.md` into a full command and option reference. Added `reference/mcp.md` covering all 14 MCP tools and input fields. Added `reference/http.md` covering Hub discovery, join, sync, project, admin, and `/mcp` endpoints. Updated VitePress sidebar navigation. Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, hidden leak scan for local paths/dev commands, Playwright homepage screenshot at 1440x900, and pushed commit 8855e9c. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

title: Website keeps native VitePress presentation
summary: DevMesh website design should stay close to the native VitePress docs presentation rather than using @theojs/lumen-driven homepage and theme overrides. Keep the homepage restrained, content-first, and aligned with the original site structure while preserving current docs content and published install guidance.
